perturbation.original_text: 4. Attendee agrees that money damages w ill not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by him and that in addition to all other remedies which may be available, BOMI will be entitled to specific perf ormance and injunctive or other equitable relief, without bond, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. All remedies available to BOMI for breach of this Agreement are cumulative and may be exercised concurrently or separately, and the exercise of any one remedy sha ll not be deemed an election of such remedy to the exclusion of other remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Attendee agrees that money damages w ill not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by him and that in addition to all other remedies which may be available, BOMI will be entitled to specific perf ormance and injunctive or other equitable relief, with bond, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. All remedies available to BOMI for breach of this Agreement are cumulative and may be exercised concurrently or separately, and the exercise of any one remedy sha ll not be deemed an election of such remedy to the exclusion of other remedies.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'without bond' to 'with bond' in Section 4 creates a direct contradiction. One version allows BOMI to seek injunctive relief without posting a bond, while the other requires it. This directly contradicts the original term regarding bond requirements for injunctive relief. It contradicts in-text, that injunctive relief can be acquired without a bond.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Attendee agrees that money damages w ill not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by him and that in addition to all other remedies which may be available, BOMI will be entitled to specific perf ormance and injunctive or other equitable relief, with bond, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. All remedies available to BOMI for breach of this Agreement are cumulative and may be exercised concurrently or separately, and the exercise of any one remedy sha ll not be deemed an election of such remedy to the exclusion of other remedies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 4 from 'without bond' to 'with bond'. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, that allows injunctive relief 'with bond' as a remedy for breach. This means the original version of Section 4 stated injunctive relief can be acquired 'without bond', and now states injunctive relief can be acquired 'with bond'. Therefore, Section 4 now contradicts itself, which is an in-text contradiction. It's a strong contradiction as it creates uncertainty and directly changes a key term. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, where different sections (in this case, different versions of the same section) provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Attendee understands that BOMI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information disclosed, and Attendee agrees that neither BOMI nor any of its officers, director s, Attendees, agents, members or attorneys will have any liability to Attendee arising from Attendee’s use of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Attendee understands that BOMI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information disclosed, and Attendee agrees that neither BOMI nor any of its officers, director s, Attendees, agents, members or attorneys will have any liability to Attendee arising from Attendee’s use of the Confidential Information, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct' to Section 7 creates a contradiction. Previously, BOMI and its representatives had no liability, but now they do under specific circumstances. This introduces in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the liability limitations, affecting how the Attendee uses the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Attendee understands that BOMI makes no representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Confidential Information disclosed, and Attendee agrees that neither BOMI nor any of its officers, director s, Attendees, agents, members or attorneys will have any liability to Attendee arising from Attendee’s use of the Confidential Information, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that BOMI has no liability arising from the Attendee's use of Confidential Information. The changed text modifies this by adding 'except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct.' This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text now states that BOMI *does* have liability under specific circumstances (gross negligence or willful misconduct), which directly conflicts with the initial statement of no liability. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed text, but is presented to highlight that this newly introduced exception directly breaks with the initial statement of no liability as it was originally written.
